title: Attributes Object
description: Provides access to attributes associated with a specific entity, Product, or Document. Also supports the creation of new attributes.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Adds a new attribute to the parent entity. If an attribute already exists on the entity with the same groupName and name already exists, this will update the existing attribute with the new value.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Returns the specified attribute using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Returns the specified attribute using the name of the attribute.

methods.name: itemsByGroup
methods.description: Returns an array of all of the attributes that belong to the specified group.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of attributes in the collection.

properties.name: groupNames
properties.description: Returns an array of strings that are all of the name of attribute groups that exist on this entity. An empty array can be returns if there are no attributes on the entity.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: Attributes.add Method
add.description: Adds a new attribute to the parent entity. If an attribute already exists on the entity with the same groupName and name already exists, this will update the existing attribute with the new value.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created attribute or null if the creation failed. If an attribute with the  same groupName and name already exists, it will return the existing attribute.

parameters.name: groupName
parameters.description: The name of the attribute group to create this attribute within.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the attribute. This must be unique with respect to other attributes in the group.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value of the attribute. The size of an attribute value is limited to 2MB (2097152 bytes). If you need to save data that is larger than 2MB you'll need to break it into pieces and save it in multiple attributes.

classType.title: Attributes.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: Attributes.item Method
item.description: Returns the specified attribute using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified attribute or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the attribute within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: Attributes.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Returns the specified attribute using the name of the attribute.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified attribute or null if no attribute exists with the specified name.

parameters.name: groupName
parameters.description: The name of the attribute group this attribute will belong to.

itemsByGroup.title: Attributes.itemsByGroup Method
itemsByGroup.description: Returns an array of all of the attributes that belong to the specified group.
itemsByGroup.return_description: Returns an array of attributes or will fail in the case where an invalid group name is specified.

count.title: Attributes.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of attributes in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = attributes_var.count

groupNames.title: Attributes.groupNames Property
groupNames.description: Returns an array of strings that are all of the name of attribute groups that exist on this entity. An empty array can be returns if there are no attributes on the entity.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = attributes_var.groupNames

isValid.title: Attributes.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = attributes_var.isValid

objectType.title: Attributes.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = attributes_var.objectType
